# LazyBridge — LLM Reference v0.3.1

Zero-boilerplate multi-provider LLM agent framework.
One class for every LLM interaction. Automatic tool schema from type hints. Composable context injection. Serializable multi-agent pipelines.

---

## Install

```
pip install lazybridge                      # core only
pip install lazybridge[anthropic]           # + Anthropic / Claude
pip install lazybridge[openai]              # + OpenAI / GPT
pip install lazybridge[google]              # + Google / Gemini
pip install lazybridge[all]                 # all providers
```

---

## Core import

```python
from lazybridge import (
    LazyAgent, LazySession, LazyTool, LazyContext,
    LazyStore, LazyRouter, GraphSchema,
    TrackLevel, Event,
)
# Advanced types:
from lazybridge.core.types import (
    CompletionResponse, CompletionRequest, StreamChunk,
    NativeTool, ThinkingConfig, SkillsConfig,
    StructuredOutputConfig, Message, Role,
)
```

---

## Providers

| Alias | Default model | Env var |
|---|---|---|
| `anthropic` / `claude` | claude-sonnet-4-6 | ANTHROPIC_API_KEY |
| `openai` / `gpt` | gpt-5.4 | OPENAI_API_KEY |
| `google` / `gemini` | gemini-2.5-flash | GOOGLE_API_KEY |
| `deepseek` | deepseek-chat | DEEPSEEK_API_KEY |

---

## Communication rules (critical)

1. Agents communicate via **return values**, NOT via LazyStore.
2. LazyStore is a **blackboard for persistent state**, not a message bus.
3. LazyContext injects strings into system prompts — it is **NOT a communication channel**.
4. `tool_runner` fallback only for tools without a callable (`ToolDefinition`/`dict`).

---

## LazyAgent — constructor

```python
LazyAgent(
    provider: str | BaseProvider,          # "anthropic"|"claude"|"openai"|"gpt"|"google"|"gemini"|"deepseek"
    *,
    name: str | None = None,               # defaults to first 8 chars of uuid
    description: str | None = None,        # used when exposed as tool
    model: str | None = None,              # overrides provider default
    system: str | None = None,             # base system prompt
    context: LazyContext | Callable[[], str] | None = None,  # injected into system at execution time
    tools: list[LazyTool | ToolDefinition | dict] | None = None,  # agent-level tools
    native_tools: list[NativeTool | str] | None = None,         # provider-managed tools (web search, etc.)
    output_schema: type | dict | None = None,                   # agent-level structured output schema
    session: LazySession | None = None,    # enables tracking, store, graph
    verbose: bool = False,
    max_retries: int = 0,
    api_key: str | None = None,
    **kwargs,
)
```

## LazyAgent — methods

```python
LazyAgent.chat(
    messages: str | list[Message | dict],
    *,
    memory: Memory | None = None,
    system: str | None = None,
    tools: list | None = None,
    native_tools: list[NativeTool | str] | None = None,
    output_schema: type | dict | None = None,
    thinking: bool | ThinkingConfig = False,
    skills: list[str] | None = None,
    stream: bool = False,
    model: str | None = None,
    max_tokens: int | None = None,
    temperature: float | None = None,
    tool_choice: str | None = None,        # "auto"|"none"|"required"|"<tool_name>"
    context: LazyContext | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
    **kwargs,
) -> CompletionResponse | Iterator[StreamChunk]

LazyAgent.achat(...)  # async — same signature → CompletionResponse | AsyncIterator[StreamChunk]

LazyAgent.loop(
    messages: str | list,
    *,
    tools: list | None = None,
    native_tools: list[NativeTool | str] | None = None,
    max_steps: int = 8,
    tool_runner: Callable[[str, dict], Any] | None = None,
    on_event: Callable[[str, Any], None] | None = None,  # events: "step"|"tool_call"|"tool_result"|"done"|"verify_rejected"
    verify: LazyAgent | Callable[[str, str], str] | None = None,  # judge: return "APPROVED..." or "RETRY: <reason>"
    max_verify: int = 3,                   # max retry attempts when verify is set
    **chat_kwargs,
) -> CompletionResponse

LazyAgent.aloop(...)  # async → CompletionResponse; verify callable may be async

LazyAgent.text(messages: str | list, **kwargs) -> str
LazyAgent.json(messages: str | list, schema: type | dict, **kwargs) -> Any
LazyAgent.atext(messages: str | list, **kwargs) -> str
LazyAgent.ajson(messages: str | list, schema: type | dict, **kwargs) -> Any

LazyAgent.as_tool(
    name: str | None = None,
    description: str | None = None,
    *,
    guidance: str | None = None,
    output_schema: type | dict | None = None,
    native_tools: list | None = None,
    system_prompt: str | None = None,
    strict: bool = False,
) -> LazyTool

# Attributes
LazyAgent.id: str           # UUID
LazyAgent.name: str
LazyAgent.description: str | None
LazyAgent._last_output: str | None  # set after each chat()/loop(); read by LazyContext.from_agent()
```

---

## LazySession

```python
LazySession(
    *,
    db: str | None = None,                 # SQLite path; None = in-memory
    tracking: TrackLevel | str = TrackLevel.BASIC,  # OFF | BASIC | VERBOSE | FULL
    console: bool = False,
)

LazySession.id: str
LazySession.store: LazyStore
LazySession.events: EventLog
LazySession.graph: GraphSchema

LazySession.gather(*coros: Awaitable) -> list[Any]  # asyncio.gather wrapper

LazySession.as_tool(
    name: str,
    description: str,
    *,
    mode: str | None = None,               # "parallel" | "chain"
    participants: list[LazyAgent | LazyTool] | None = None,
    combiner: str = "concat",              # parallel only: "concat" | "last"
    entry_agent: LazyAgent | None = None,
    guidance: str | None = None,
) -> LazyTool

LazySession.to_json() -> str
LazySession.from_json(text: str, **kwargs) -> LazySession  # classmethod
```

### TrackLevel / Event

```python
TrackLevel.OFF | BASIC | VERBOSE | FULL
Event.MODEL_REQUEST | MODEL_RESPONSE | TOOL_CALL | TOOL_RESULT | TOOL_ERROR
Event.AGENT_START | AGENT_FINISH | LOOP_STEP
Event.MESSAGES | SYSTEM_CONTEXT | STREAM_CHUNK  # VERBOSE only
```

---

## LazyTool

```python
LazyTool.from_function(
    func: Callable,
    *,
    name: str | None = None,
    description: str | None = None,
    guidance: str | None = None,
    schema_mode: ToolSchemaMode = ToolSchemaMode.SIGNATURE,
    strict: bool = False,
    schema_builder: ToolSchemaBuilder | None = None,
    schema_llm: Any | None = None,
) -> LazyTool

LazyTool.from_agent(
    agent: LazyAgent,
    *,
    name: str | None = None,
    description: str | None = None,
    guidance: str | None = None,
    output_schema: type | dict | None = None,
    native_tools: list | None = None,
    system_prompt: str | None = None,
    strict: bool = False,
) -> LazyTool  # schema always {"task": str}

LazyTool.compile(schema_llm: Any = None) -> LazyTool
LazyTool.run(arguments: dict, parent: LazyAgent | None = None) -> Any
LazyTool.arun(arguments: dict, parent: LazyAgent | None = None) -> Any
LazyTool.definition() -> ToolDefinition
LazyTool.specialize(name: str|None=None, description: str|None=None, guidance: str|None=None, schema_mode: ToolSchemaMode|None=None, strict: bool|None=None) -> LazyTool
```

---

## LazyContext

```python
LazyContext.from_text(text: str) -> LazyContext
LazyContext.from_function(fn: Callable[[], str]) -> LazyContext
LazyContext.from_store(
    store: LazyStore,
    *,
    keys: list[str] | None = None,
    prefix: str = "[store context]",
) -> LazyContext
LazyContext.from_agent(
    agent: LazyAgent,
    *,
    prefix: str | None = None,
) -> LazyContext  # reads agent._last_output; "" if agent not yet run

LazyContext.merge(*contexts: LazyContext) -> LazyContext
LazyContext.__add__(other) -> LazyContext   # ctx1 + ctx2
LazyContext.build() -> str
LazyContext.__call__() -> str              # alias for build()
```

**Lazy context chaining — wire topology before execution:**
```python
# Declare agents and contexts — nothing runs yet, zero side effects
researcher   = LazyAgent("anthropic", name="researcher")
fact_checker = LazyAgent("openai",    name="fact_checker",
    context=LazyContext.from_agent(researcher))
writer       = LazyAgent("anthropic", name="writer",
    context=(
        LazyContext.from_agent(researcher,    prefix="[Research]")
        + LazyContext.from_agent(fact_checker, prefix="[Fact-check]")
        + LazyContext.from_text("Write in plain English, max 300 words.")
    ))

# Execute in order — from_agent() pulls _last_output at call time
researcher.loop("Climate research findings this month?")
fact_checker.chat("Verify. Flag unsourced claims.")
result = writer.chat("Write the briefing.")   # sees both agents' outputs
```

Key properties:
- **Pull model**: Agent A never sends anything. Agent B reads A._last_output when B runs.
- **Safe before run**: `from_agent()` returns `""` if agent hasn't run yet — no exception.
- **Composable**: `from_agent() + from_store() + from_text() + from_function()` all work together.
- **Inspectable**: `writer.context()` shows exactly what the LLM will receive.

---

## LazyStore

```python
LazyStore(db: str | None = None)           # None = in-memory; str = SQLite path

LazyStore.write(key: str, value: Any, *, agent_id: str | None = None) -> None
LazyStore.read(key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any
LazyStore.read_entry(key: str) -> StoreEntry | None
LazyStore.read_all() -> dict[str, Any]
LazyStore.read_by_agent(agent_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]
LazyStore.keys() -> list[str]
LazyStore.entries() -> list[StoreEntry]
LazyStore.delete(key: str) -> None
LazyStore.clear() -> None
LazyStore.to_text(keys: list[str] | None = None) -> str

# Async API
LazyStore.awrite / aread / aread_all / akeys

# StoreEntry fields: key, value, agent_id, written_at (datetime, UTC)
```

---

## LazyRouter

```python
LazyRouter(
    condition: Callable[[Any], str],
    routes: dict[str, LazyAgent],
    name: str = "router",
    default: str | None = None,
)
LazyRouter.route(value: Any) -> LazyAgent
LazyRouter.aroute(value: Any) -> LazyAgent  # awaits condition if coroutine
LazyRouter.agent_names -> list[str]
```

---

## CompletionResponse

```python
.content: str
.thinking: str | None
.tool_calls: list[ToolCall]
.stop_reason: str
.model: str | None
.usage: UsageStats             # .input_tokens, .output_tokens, .cost_usd
.raw: Any
.parsed: Any                   # populated when output_schema is set
.validated: bool | None
.validation_error: str | None
.grounding_sources: list[GroundingSource]  # .url, .title, .snippet
.web_search_queries: list[str]
.verify_log: list[str]             # judge verdicts that rejected, in order; [] if verify=None or approved first try
.raise_if_failed() -> None
```

## Memory

```python
Memory()
Memory.from_history(messages: list[dict]) -> Memory
mem.history   # list[dict]
len(mem)
mem.clear()
```

## NativeTool

```python
NativeTool.WEB_SEARCH | CODE_EXECUTION | FILE_SEARCH | COMPUTER_USE | GOOGLE_SEARCH | GOOGLE_MAPS
```

---

## Type annotation → JSON Schema (SIGNATURE mode)

| Python | JSON Schema |
|--------|-------------|
| `str` | `{"type":"string"}` |
| `int` | `{"type":"integer"}` |
| `float` | `{"type":"number"}` |
| `bool` | `{"type":"boolean"}` |
| `list[X]` | `{"type":"array","items":<X>}` |
| `dict` | `{"type":"object"}` |
| `Optional[X]` / `X | None` | `{"anyOf":[<X>,{"type":"null"}]}` |
| `Literal["a","b"]` | `{"enum":["a","b"]}` |
| `MyModel` (Pydantic) | `model_json_schema()` |
| `Annotated[X, "desc"]` | `{<X>, "description":"desc"}` |
| `Any` / unannotated | `{}` |

Parameters are **required** when no default; optional when `param=default`.

---

## Pattern decision tree

```
Need one agent to drive others?
├─ YES → Pattern A (Hierarchy): orchestrator.loop(..., tools=[agent_a.as_tool(), ...])
└─ NO
   ├─ Agents run independently in parallel?
   │   ├─ YES, auto-wired → sess.as_tool(mode="parallel")
   │   └─ YES, decoupled  → Pattern C (LazyStore + LazyContext)
   ├─ Agents run sequentially, each feeds next?
   │   └─ YES → sess.as_tool(mode="chain") or manual: b.chat(a.chat(task).content)
   ├─ Need to branch on condition?
   │   └─ YES → Pattern D: LazyRouter(condition=fn, routes={...})
   └─ Need to expose whole pipeline as a single tool?
             → Pattern E: sess.as_tool(mode="chain", participants=[...])
```

### Which method on a single agent?

```
Need tool use?       → agent.loop() / agent.aloop()
Need structured out? → agent.json(msg, schema=MyModel)
Need just text?      → agent.text(msg)
Need full response?  → agent.chat(msg)
Need streaming?      → agent.chat(msg, stream=True)
```

---

## Pattern A — Hierarchy (runnable example)

```python
from lazybridge import LazyAgent

researcher = LazyAgent("anthropic", name="researcher",
    description="Searches and returns factual summaries.",
    system="You are a research assistant.")
analyst = LazyAgent("openai", name="analyst",
    description="Analyses data and identifies trends.",
    system="You are a data analyst.")
orchestrator = LazyAgent("anthropic",
    system="Coordinate research and analysis tasks.")

result = orchestrator.loop(
    "Prepare a report on the current state of open-source LLMs.",
    tools=[researcher.as_tool(), analyst.as_tool()],
    max_steps=10,
)
print(result.content)
```

---

## Pattern B — Parallel (runnable example)

```python
from lazybridge import LazyAgent, LazySession

sess = LazySession(tracking="verbose")
research_tool = sess.as_tool(
    "multi_domain_research",
    "Research transformer architecture, market trends, and AI regulation simultaneously.",
    mode="parallel",
    participants=[
        LazyAgent("anthropic", name="tech_researcher",   session=sess),
        LazyAgent("openai",    name="market_researcher", session=sess),
        LazyAgent("anthropic", name="legal_researcher",  session=sess),
    ],
)
editor = LazyAgent("anthropic", name="editor", session=sess)
report = editor.loop(
    "Research AI across technology, market, and regulation. Write an executive summary.",
    tools=[research_tool],
)
print(report.content)
```

---

## Pattern C — Network / Store (runnable example)

```python
from lazybridge import LazyAgent, LazyContext, LazyStore

store = LazyStore(db="pipeline.db")
collector = LazyAgent("anthropic", name="collector")
analyst   = LazyAgent("openai", name="analyst",
                      context=LazyContext.from_store(store, keys=["papers"]))
writer    = LazyAgent("anthropic", name="writer",
                      context=LazyContext.from_store(store, keys=["papers", "analysis"]))

collector.loop("Collect the top 5 AI papers published this week")
store.write("papers", collector._last_output, agent_id=collector.id)
analyst.chat("Identify the 3 most impactful findings.")
store.write("analysis", analyst._last_output, agent_id=analyst.id)
report = writer.chat("Write a professional newsletter section.")
print(report.content)
```

---

## Pattern D — Router (runnable example)

```python
from lazybridge import LazyAgent, LazyRouter, LazySession

sess = LazySession()
drafter   = LazyAgent("anthropic", name="drafter",   session=sess)
reviewer  = LazyAgent("openai",    name="reviewer",  session=sess)
publisher = LazyAgent("anthropic", name="publisher", session=sess)

router = LazyRouter(
    condition=lambda r: "publisher" if "APPROVED" in r.upper() else "reviewer",
    routes={"publisher": publisher, "reviewer": reviewer},
    default="reviewer",
)
draft = drafter.chat("Write a short blog post about AI safety.")
next_agent = router.route(draft.content)
result = next_agent.chat("Process this content: " + draft.content)
print(result.content)
```

---

## Pattern E — Pipeline as Tool (runnable example)

```python
from lazybridge import LazyAgent, LazySession

inner_sess = LazySession()
pipeline_tool = inner_sess.as_tool(
    "research_and_summarise",
    "Researches a topic and returns a concise summary.",
    mode="chain",
    participants=[
        LazyAgent("anthropic", name="researcher", session=inner_sess),
        LazyAgent("openai",    name="summariser", session=inner_sess),
    ],
)
orchestrator = LazyAgent("anthropic")
result = orchestrator.loop(
    "Prepare reports on: 1) quantum computing 2) fusion energy 3) space debris",
    tools=[pipeline_tool],
    max_steps=12,
)
print(result.content)
```

---

## Variant 4 — Fully typed multi-provider pipeline (complex example)

Three-layer pipeline: parallel Google web-search agents → Anthropic risk analyst → OpenAI report writer. Every agent owns `output_schema`; the chain calls `json()` automatically at each step. Zero manual type conversion.

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel
from lazybridge import LazyAgent, LazySession
from lazybridge.core.types import NativeTool

class MarketBriefing(BaseModel):
    sector: str
    key_trends: list[str]
    top_risks: list[str]
    outlook: str

class RiskProfile(BaseModel):
    overall_risk: str
    sector_risks: dict[str, str]
    recommendations: list[str]

class InvestmentReport(BaseModel):
    executive_summary: str
    top_opportunities: list[str]
    risk_summary: str
    conclusion: str

# Layer 1: three Google agents run in parallel, each produces a MarketBriefing
market_sess = LazySession(tracking="basic", console=True)
LazyAgent("google", name="tech_researcher",    session=market_sess,
          native_tools=[NativeTool.WEB_SEARCH], output_schema=MarketBriefing,
          system="Technology analyst — AI, semiconductors, cloud. Output ONLY valid JSON.")
LazyAgent("google", name="energy_researcher",  session=market_sess,
          native_tools=[NativeTool.WEB_SEARCH], output_schema=MarketBriefing,
          system="Energy analyst — renewables, oil & gas, grid. Output ONLY valid JSON.")
LazyAgent("google", name="biotech_researcher", session=market_sess,
          native_tools=[NativeTool.WEB_SEARCH], output_schema=MarketBriefing,
          system="Biotech analyst — pharma, genomics, medical devices. Output ONLY valid JSON.")

parallel_market_tool = market_sess.as_tool(
    "parallel_market_research",
    "Three Google agents research tech, energy, and biotech in parallel.",
    mode="parallel",
)

# Layers 2 + 3: risk analyst and report writer
analysis_sess = LazySession(tracking="basic", console=True)
risk_analyst  = LazyAgent("anthropic", name="risk_analyst",  session=analysis_sess,
                           output_schema=RiskProfile,
                           system="Senior risk analyst. Output ONLY valid JSON.")
report_writer = LazyAgent("openai",    name="report_writer", session=analysis_sess,
                           output_schema=InvestmentReport,
                           system="CFA report writer. Output ONLY valid JSON.")

# Full pipeline: parallel tool → risk analyst → report writer — zero def
pipeline = LazySession(tracking="basic", console=True).as_tool(
    "fully_structured_pipeline",
    "Parallel Google research → Anthropic risk assessment → OpenAI report.",
    mode="chain",
    participants=[parallel_market_tool, risk_analyst, report_writer],
)

result = pipeline.run({"task": "Analyse investment opportunities in AI infrastructure Q2 2025."})
print(result)
```

---

## Common mistakes

- **Sharing output via LazyStore instead of return values**: agents communicate via return values (tool results, `.content`). Store is for persistence, not messaging.
- **Calling `LazyContext.from_agent()` before the agent has run**: returns `""`. Run the agent first, then build the context reader.
- **Using `loop()` when no tools are needed**: use `chat()` instead. `loop()` is for tool use.
- **Not setting `output_schema`** when you need structured output from the LLM: pass a Pydantic model to get `.parsed` populated and `.validated = True`.
- **Parallel execution requires async**: `sess.gather()` runs coroutines concurrently. Sync `chat()` / `loop()` calls are sequential even in parallel patterns — use `achat()` / `aloop()`.
- **`mode="chain"` in `sess.as_tool()`** passes each agent's output as the next agent's task string. If the first participant is a `LazyTool` (not a `LazyAgent`), it runs via `.run({"task": ...})`.

---

## GraphSchema (serialization)

```python
# Auto-populated when agents are created with session=sess
print(sess.graph.to_json())   # pipeline topology for GUI or debugging

# Save / load
sess.graph.save("pipeline.yaml")
g = GraphSchema.from_file("pipeline.yaml")
```

---

## Custom Providers (stable extension point)

Subclass `BaseProvider` to integrate any LLM backend. The interface is **stable**.

```python
from lazybridge.core.providers.base import BaseProvider
from lazybridge.core.types import CompletionRequest, CompletionResponse, StreamChunk, UsageStats

class MyProvider(BaseProvider):
    default_model = "my-model-v1"

    def _init_client(self, **kwargs) -> None:
        import my_sdk
        self._client = my_sdk.Client(api_key=self.api_key)

    def complete(self, request: CompletionRequest) -> CompletionResponse:
        resp = self._client.generate(
            model=self._resolve_model(request),
            messages=...,           # convert request.messages
            system=request.system,
        )
        return CompletionResponse(
            content=resp.text,
            model=self._resolve_model(request),
            usage=UsageStats(input_tokens=resp.in_tokens, output_tokens=resp.out_tokens),
            raw=resp,
        )

    def stream(self, request: CompletionRequest):
        for chunk in self._client.stream(...):
            yield StreamChunk(delta=chunk.text)
        yield StreamChunk(stop_reason="end_turn", is_final=True,
                          usage=UsageStats(output_tokens=...))

    async def acomplete(self, request: CompletionRequest) -> CompletionResponse:
        ...   # async version of complete()

    async def astream(self, request: CompletionRequest):
        ...   # async generator version of stream()
```

```python
from lazybridge import LazyAgent
agent = LazyAgent(MyProvider(api_key="..."))
print(agent.chat("hello").content)
```

Stable helpers available in all subclasses:
- `_resolve_model(request)` — request.model → self.model → default_model
- `_check_native_tools(tools)` — filter unsupported NativeTool values with warning
- `_compute_cost(model, input_tokens, output_tokens)` — override for cost tracking

Rules:
- Return `CompletionResponse` with `content` always set (even if empty string).
- Final `StreamChunk` must have `is_final=True` and `stop_reason` set.
- Never mutate `request`. Never block inside `acomplete`/`astream`.
- Let exceptions propagate — the `Executor` retry layer handles them.

Full example with Ollama: `lazy_wiki/bot/11_custom_provider.md`

---

## Source

LazyBridge v0.3.1 — https://github.com/selvaz/LazyBridge
Wiki: lazy_wiki/bot/ (00_quickref.md, 08_patterns.md, 11_custom_provider.md, INDEX.md)
